System1: Reset the root password of the system1 server to « password123 ».
Create a backup file named « /root/backup.tar.bz2 ». The backup file should contain the content of « /usr/local » and should be zipped with bzip2 compression format.
Ensure selinux is in enforcing mode. If it is not, change selinux to enforcing mode.
Upgrade the kernel to, and configure the system to Start with the previous kernel.
Configure a task : write the message hello to rsyslog every monday at 17h.30.
Find the files owned by the user student, and copy it to catalog : /opt/dir.
Write a shell script that finds files located in /etc and whose size is between 2bytes and 10M.
Find the rows that contain /bin/bash from file /etc/passwd, and write it to the file /tmp/testfile.
On system2, configure Europe as the time zone.
Configure system1 to be a ntp client for system2.
Add user1, user2 and user3. The additional group of the two users : user2, user3 is the admin group. Password : redhat.
Add admin group and set gid=6000.
Create a shared directory /home/admins... (characteristics: group owner, SGID, permissions).
Copy /etc/passwd to /var/tmp/pass. Set permissions: Owner root, group root, no execute for anyone, user1 rw, user2 none (ACLs).
Open the ip_forward, and take effect permanently.
Add the service smtp to your firewall configuration.
Autofs: mount home folder for formateur1 from server2.
Make balanced profile as your default profile.
Container logserver: Create from rsyslog image, configure with systemd for user student, auto-start, journal storage, automount /var/log/journal.
From your container, write the text « hello tekup » to logs.
System2: Create a 512M partition (/dev/sdb of 2GB), ext4, mounted automatically under /mnt/data1.
Make a swap partition having 100MB (/dev/sdb). Make automatically usable at system boot time.
LVM: Create vg (5GB, PE=32M), Lvol1 (1256M, ext4, /mnt/lv1), Lvol2 (10 LE, xfs, /mnt/lvo2).
Reduce the size of Lvol1 to 500M.
Extend the size of lvol2 to 620M.
VDO: Create volume class1_vdo (logical size 30GB) on /dev/sdc, format xfs, mount on /class1_mnt.

Server: Set the root password to tekup.
Setup IP addr 172.25.2.11, subnet 255.255.255.0, GW 172.25.2.254, DNS 172.25.254.254, hostname serverx.example.com.
Create lv1 (2G, xfs) on /dev/sdb, mount permanently under /lv1.
Create VDO volume on /dev/sdc (logical size 200G, ext4, mount /vdo).
Create Stratis fs named fs, pool name is pool on /dev/sdd.
Create VG vg-wt (50 PE, size 8M) on /dev/sde, create lv1 (30 LE, ext4, mount /mnt/data).
Create 1G swap on /dev/sde, available after reboot.
Find files owned by student, copy to /opt/dir.
Shell script: Find files under /home (perm 664, size < 10M), copy to /opt/dir.
Cronjob for student2: date command to file every 3 minutes (noon to midnight), message 'finished' in logs.
Client: SSH key exchange for student account to second machine.
Copy list of Booleans to /bool file.
Create /tic directory (sticky bit, only owner/root can delete).

metadonnees.Enseignant: Sahar Ben Yaala & Hayet Slimani & Moez Elarfaoui

exercices.Exercice 1: QCM: Expressions régulières et commandes shell (digit, killall, chage, notation).
exercices.Exercice 2: Ordonnancement (PCTER/SRTF, Round Robin, Temps de latence).
exercices.Exercice 3: Programmation C: Fork et affichage PID/PPID (arbre de processus).
exercices.Exercice 4: Commandes Linux sur fichier telephone.txt (grep, sed, awk).

taches: Créez lv1 (25 LE, ext4, temp mount /lv1).
Etendre lv2 de 2G (extension du VG possible).

Access web pages from second machine.

Default pass expire 100 days, UID start 1500.

questions: Password policy: min 8 chars, 1 digit, 1 special.

Yum sources (BaseOS, AppStream).

remarque: Ce document contient les questions suivies des commandes de correction.

questions: Set the network configuration (IP, Subnet, Gateway, DNS, Hostname).
Configure users and groups to have a minimum uid and gid of 1100.
Set the password policy: minimum 8 characters length, 3 categories.
Create groups redhat and tekup with respective gid 1300 and 1345.
Create users john and alex with specific attributes (UIDs, comments, groups).
Configure john's password to expire by 2021, minimum age 7. Expire alex's password.
Create directory “/home/tekup” with specific group permissions and ownership.
Files created in tekup have a maximum of 400 as permissions.
Create a 1G swap partition automounted by system boot.
Create a 150M ext4 partition automounted at “/mnt/ext”.
Create an lvm called lv0 (200M) under a VG (PE 4M, total 400), formatted xfs, mounted at “/mnt/lv0”.
Copy all files owned by “user” under “/opt/dir”.
Create a file containing all packages installed that starts with ‘k’.
Save the system services under a file called services.
Change every ‘.’ in services with ‘:’ and save to services_changed.
Save the difference between services and services_changed.
Setup a new repository to download packages locally from ‘/var/ftp/repo’.
Configure the system to use ldap configuration via server labipa.example.com with tls.
Configure autofs for ldapuser1 home folder mount.
Set the system to sync time with server 192.168.4.200.

Server1: Create the user named eric with no interactive shell. His home is /base/eric.
Set cronjob for user guillaume to execute /bin/echo ‘hi’ at 14:23.
Create a 2G swap partition which take effect automatically at boot-start.
Create a logical volume named vo (size= 300M, LE=4M). Use ext4 and /vo as mount point.
Extend vo so that the new size will be between 319M and 323M.
Create a backup file named /root/backup.tar.bz2 of /usr/local using bzip2.
Create a new logical volume 'database' (50 extents, PE=16MB) in 'datastore' VG, ext3, mounted under /mnt/database.
Create a shared directory /home/admins (group adminuser, SGID, read/write for group).
Resize Logical Volume lv1 from 100MB to 500MB without losing data.
Create a user named alex (uid 1234, password alex111). Files created should have permissions rw- - - - r - -.
Enable the boolean container_manage_cgroup permanently.
Add 3 new users alice, bob, charles to group marketing. Configure directory /marketing permissions.
Set the system time zone and configure NTP.
Configure password aging for charles (expire in 60 days).
Lock bobs account.
Find all setuid files and save to /testresults/setuid.list.
Create a periodic job as alice (every 5 mins, Sun/Wed, 3am-4am).
Set the system SELinux mode to permissive.
Create a firewall rule to drop all traffic from 10.10.10.0.
Enable SSH passwordless connection for dev user between server1 and server2.
Server 2: Set the umask so that new files are only readable and writeable by the owner.
Create three users (Derek, Tom, Kenny) in instructors group. Modify Tom (no shell, expire 10 days).
Download and configure apache to serve index.html from /var/web on port 94.
Find all log messages containing "ACPI" in /var/log/messages, export to file, archive /var/log.
Modify the GRUB timeout to 1 second.
Create a daily cron job at 4:27PM for Derek.
Configure time.nist.gov as the only NTP Server.
Create a new logical volume (LV-A) with 30 extents (PE=32M), mount on /mnt.
Create a container logserver from rsyslog image.
Configure the container with systemd services by user “derek” (auto-start).
Configure host journal to store all journal across reboot.
Set the recommended profile as tuned profile.

Change your target to graphical target for this session.
Configure Host Name, IP, Gateway, DNS (hostname: clientnode.example.com).

Find rows containing « strat » from /usr/share/dict/words to /tmp/testfile.

Rename user bob to maninder and change UID to 5460.

Configure all users passwords expire after 500 days.
Configure password complexity (at least one capital letter, two digits).

Configure httpd to run on port 82.

Create Directory /home/manager (group manager, full perm for root/group, SGID).

Locate files of owner 'gabriel' and copy to /root/result.
Find all files of root with setuid and copy to /root/output. Compress with bzip2.
List lines with string « ip » from /usr/share/dict/words to /root/found.
Create Logical Volume 'lv' (5 PEs, PE=32MB, ext4) mounted on /mnt/lv. Resize between 225M-275M.
Create Logical Volume 'vol' (100 PEs, PE=8MB, vfat) mounted on /vol.

Networking: Configure Machine 1 (client.example.com) and Machine 2 (server.example.com) network settings.
Machine 1: Set multi-user target as default and reboot.
Find lines containing 'seismic' in /usr/share/dict/words, save to /root/wordlist.

Create groups linuxadm (GID 5000), dba. Add David to linuxadm. Change linuxadm to sysadm (GID 6000).

Copy services list to /root/services. Set specific permissions.
Create container logserver (rsyslog) as user10 with systemd and journal automount.
Configure http web server on port 87.
Script to find files owned by user10 (size 10K-10M).
Machine 2: Write script backup.sh to find/copy files < 2M.
Script to backup /etc nightly via cron (11PM, except Sunday).
LVM: Create vgfs (PE 16MB), ext4vol and xfsvol (80MB each). Mount persistently. Extend xfsvol.

Create VDO volume vdo1 (logical 16GB, xfs) on sdc. Mount /xfsvdo1.
Create periodic job for user (date to file). Remove bob's cron access.
Submit job as user100 for specific date.
